title: Satteppa Thabagouda Umarani vs Thabagouda Satteppa Umarani on 7 September, 2012
body: Satteppa Thabagouda Umarani vs Thabagouda Satteppa Umarani on 7 September, 2012 Author: Huluvadi G.Ramesh Bench: Huluvadi G. Ramesh 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA CIRCUIT BENCH AT DHARWAD DATED THIS THE 7TH DAY OF SEPTEMBER, 2012 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE HULUVADI G. RAMESH R.S.A. No.5985/2010 c/w R.S.A.No.6065/2010 (PARTN. & SEP.POSSN.) IN RSA.5985/2010 BETWEEN: SHRI SATTEPPA THABAGOUDA UMARANI AGE: 51 YEARS, OCC: AGRICULTURE, R/O: HARUGERI-591 220, TQ: RAIBAG, DIST: BELGAUM. ... APPELLANT (By SHRI B S KAMATE, ADV.) AND 1. THABAGOUDA SATTEPPA UMARANI AGE: 76 YEARS, OCC: AGRICULTURE, R/O: HARUGERI, TQ: RAIBAG, DIST: BELGAUM 2. DUNDAPPA THABAGOUDA UMARANI AGE: 29 YEARS, OCC: AGRICULTURE, R/O: HARUGERI, TQ: RAIBAG, DIST: BELGAUM. ... RESPONDENTS (BY SRI.RAGHUVEER P. KALWAR, ADV. FOR R.1 AND R.4, SRI.DEEPAK S. KULKARNI, ADV. FOR R.2, R.3, R.5 AND R.6) THIS RSA IS FILED U/S. 100 OF CPC . AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT & DECREE DTD:16-08-2010 PASSED IN R.A.NO.885/2009 ON THE FILE OF THE PRINCIPAL DISTRICT JUDGE, BELGAUM, DISMISSING THE APPEAL, FILED AGAINST THE JUDGMENT DTD:29-08-2009 AND THE DECREE PASSED IN O.S.NO.132/2008 ON THE FILE OF THE CIVIL JUDGE (SR.DN) RAIBAG, DECREEING THE SUIT FILED FOR PARTITION AND SEPARATE POSSESSION, AND ETC. 3 IN R.S.A.6065/2010: BETWEEN SHRI.SATTEPPA THABAGOUDA UMARANI, AGE:51 YEARS, OCC:AGRICULTURE, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220 TALUK: RAIBAG, DIST: BELGAUM. ... APPELLANT (By SHRI. B S KAMATE, ADV.) AND 1. SRI.THABAGOUDA SATTEPPA UMARANI, AGE: 76 YEARS, OCC: AGRICULTURE, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220, TALUK: RAIBAG, DIST: BELGAUM 2. SMT.NEELAWWA W/O THABAGOUDA UMARANI, AGE: 71 YEARS, OCC: HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220, TALUK: RAIBAG, DIST: BELGAUM 3. SRI.NARASAGOUDA THABAGOUDA UMARANI, AGE :45 YEARS, OCC:AGRICULTURE, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220. TALUK : RAIBAG, DIST:BELGAUM. 4. SRI.PARAGOUDA THABAGOUDA UMARANI, AGE :41 YEARS, OCC:AGRICULTURE, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220 TALUK : RAIBAG, DIST:BELGAUM. 5. MISS.SARASWATI D/O THABAGOUDA UMARANI, AGE :37 YEARS, OCC:HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220. TALUK : RAIBAG, DIST:BELGAUM. 4 6. MISS. MAHADEVI D/O THABAGOUDA UMARANI, AGE: 36 YEARS, OCC:HOUSEHOLD WORK, R/O HARUGERI - 591 220 TALUK:RAIBAG, DIST:BELGAUM. 7. SRI.SANJEEV KRISHNAGOUDA PATIL, OCC: NIL, R/O SADALAGA, TAL: CHIKODI, NOW RESIDENT OF HARUGERI-591220. TALUK: RAIBAG, DIST:BELGAUM 8. THE MANAGING DIRECTOR, VASANT DADA SHETAKARI SAHAKARI, SAKKARE KARAKHANA LTD, SANGLI, MAHADEV NAGAR, SANGLI. (MAHARASTRA STATE). ... RESPONDENTS (BY SRI. DEEPAK S. KULKARNI, ADV. FOR R.2, R.4, R.5 AND R.6, RAGHUVEER P. KALWAR, ADV. FOR R.1 AND R.3) THIS RSA IS FILED U/ S. 100 OF CPC . AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT & DECREE DTD:16-08-2010 PASSED BY THE PRINCIPAL DISTRICT JUDGE, BELGAUM, IN CROSS OBJECTIONS UNNUMBERED FILED IN R.A.NO.885/2009 REVERSING THE JUDGMENT AND DECREE DATED 29.8.2009 PASSED BY THE LEARNED CIVIL JUDGE (SR.DN), RAIBAG, IN O.S.NO.28/2009, AND ETC. THESE RSAs COMING ON FOR HEARING THIS DAY, THE COURT, DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING COMMON JUDGMENT: 5 JUDGMENT These two appeals have been filed by 2nd defendant in O.S.No.132/2008 being aggrieved by the judgment and decree passed by the Principal District Judge, Belgaum. The appellant is the 2nd defendant in O. S.132 /08 before the Civil Judge (Sr.Dn), Raibag. In the said judgment rendered on 29.8.09, the trial Court having held that the suit properties were joint family properties of the plaintiffs and defendants, notwithstanding the fact that there was separate cultivation of R.S.No.605 of Harugeri by the appellant, directed partition of plaintiffs' share in the suit properties holding that each of the plaintiffs was entitled to 1/8th share. The Trial Court also directed for partition and separate share by metes and bounds. 2. Another suit O.S.No.28/2009 filed by the plaintiffs against defendants seeking declaration and injunction with regard to sugarcane amount which has been drawn by this 2nd defendant in O. S.132 /2008, came to be dismissed. However, the plaintiffs and other 6 defendants in O. S.28 /09 instead of filing a separate appeal, have filed cross-objections as against the Judgment & Decree passed in O. S.132 /08 which came to be tried along with R.A.885/05 which was preferred by the 2nd defendant in O. S.132 /2008. 3. Both the matters were tried and cross- objections filed by the plaintiffs was allowed and the decree passed in O. S.132 /08 by the Civil Judge (Sr.Dn), Raibag, was confirmed recognizing the determination of rights of the parties by metes and bounds and also the judgment and decree passed by the Civil Judge (Sr.Dn), Raibag, dismissing O. S.28 /09 was set aside and directed the accounts be rendered with regard to sugarcane grown in the suit lands and supplied to the sugar factory by any of the parties to the suit. Against the judgment and decree passed in O. S.132 /2008, the present appellant/defendant filed R.A.885/2009 before the Principal District Judge, Belgaum. In the said appeal, plaintiffs in O. S.28 /2009 filed cross objections against the 7 judgment and decree passed in O. S.28 /2009. The Lower Appellate Court dismissed the appeal filed by the appellant and allowed the cross-objections filed by the plaintiffs in O. S.28 /2009. Therefore, the appellant has filed R.S.A.6065/2010. 4. At the time of admission of R.S.A.5985/10, the following substantial question of law has been raised: "Whether the Courts below have committed an error in holding that the suit filed for partition and separate possession is maintainable, when it was pleaded partial partition and proved exclusive possession of the land R.S.No.604/1A by the appellant/Defendant No.2?" A further substantial question of law that would arise for consideration in this appeal is: "Whether the order of the lower appellate Court to maintain the accounts for distribution of profits arising out of sugarcane crops amongst the heirs is maintainable in the suit schedule property held by the appellant herein/ defendant-2?" In R.S.A.6065/2010 this Court has raised the following substantial question of law" 8 "Whether Lower Appellate Court has erred in law in passing the orders on the Cross Objections filed by the Respondents 1 to 6 which was not maintainable?" Further another substantial question of law that would arise in RSA.6065/10 is: "Whether the order of Lower Appellate Court is bad in law for allowing the cross objections filed by Plaintiffs 1 and 6? 5. The contention of the learned Counsel for the appellant with regard to the findings of the trial Court and lower appellate Court is, though the trial Court is justified in dismissing the suit filed by the plaintiff and other defendants in O. S.132 /2008, the finding with regard to maintenance of accounts and sharing of the amount obtained from the sugar factory cannot be sustained. The lower appellate court, without application of mind and without understanding the implication of the share given to the 2nd defendant in the above suit, has proceeded to pass an order for partition which is erroneous. Further according to the learned trial judge, the property in question i.e. Sy.No.604/1A measuring 5 acres 9 6 guntas was exclusively given to this appellant. The family partition has taken place in the year 1991 and since then the appellant is residing separately in the other property itself along with his wife and children and there is recognition of his rights and interest in the property. The order of appellate Court holding that there is partial partition is erroneous as the appellate court has overlooked the admissions made by the plaintiffs and other defendants. Accordingly, he has sought to dismiss the suit as already the rights of the appellant have been recognized by the parties. As the property held by the appellant is fetching higher rate as it is adjacent to the main road, now having an eye on the property, the plaintiff No.1 being the father of the sole appellant, has filed the suit to knock of the property and to dispossess this appellant from enjoying the valuable property which has been given to his share exclusively determining his share. 6. Per contra, learned Counsel for respondents have stated that the property which is held by the joint 10 family consisting of plaintiffs and other defendants other than the appellant is not valuable. There was no proper partition. Only family arrangement had been made. As there is differences between family members, the appellant is residing separately as a matter of convenience and no share has been given to him exclusively by way of partial partition and nowhere it is recognized that the finding of both the courts below on the point of partition has to be accepted and even the finding given by the lower appellate court with regard to entitlement of the right in the income fetched from sale of sugarcane crop to all the parties is proper. Accordingly, he has sought for disposal of these appeals stating that no substantial question of law would arise for consideration. 7. At the outset, the learned Counsel appearing for the appellant submitted that he would be satisfied if in lieu of other share in other properties, he is given the property which is held by him, i.e. to the extent of 5 acres 06 guntas in Sy.No.604/1A, since already his right has 11 been recognized and ever since there is partial partition in the year 1991 he has been residing separately. 8. As a matter of fact finding, the trial Court as well as the lower appellate Court have opined that the appellant himself has agreed that the property held by him is a joint family and ancestral property. In so far as the contention of partial partition taken by the appellant is concerned, the trial Court as well as the lower appellate Court, looking into the documents namely, record of rights, have noted that property in Sy.No.508/1 also stands in the name of this appellant apart from Sy.No.604/1A, for which he has no explanation. Except the entry in the record of rights, no other material is produced to show that there is a partial partition. Sy.Nos.606/7 and 606/2 stands in the name of Paragouda Thabagouda Umarani and all other property stands in the name of plaintiff No.1. Both the Courts below have formed an opinion that the evidence on behalf of the plaintiffs as well as the defendants reveals that the suit schedule 12 properties are joint family properties. Even with respect to the house property, name of the plaintiff No.1 has been entered. Both the Courts below on the ground that there is no material to discard the assertion of the plaintiffs and other defendants, have formed an opinion that the properties including the properties held by the appellant herein, are joint family ancestral properties and that there is no separate partial partition except that the property was held separately by the appellant as a matter of convenience and that mere possession is not a clear proof of partition. The said finding of both the Courts below cannot be found fault with. 9. So far as allowing the cross objections filed by the plaintiffs and other defendants is concerned, what is being noted is, the property in Sy.No.604/1A was given to the appellant herein to enjoy exclusively. Even assuming that there was no such partial partition till arrangement is conclusively made, any income enjoyed by the appellant cannot be made a subject matter to calculate mesne 13 profits, as is admitted by the plaintiff and other defendants. It was given to him as a family arrangement and he had been enjoying and there is no sharing of income in the property. Even the appellant herein also was not getting any share or interest from other properties held by the joint family. Hence, the finding of the lower appellate Court that there shall be sharing of income out of the sugarcane proceeds is reversed accordingly. 10. So far as the calculation of the Court below that plaintiffs and defendants are entitled for 1/8th share. appears to be erroneous, since Thabagouda Satteppa Umarani and Neelawwa being husband and wife, they get one share and other family heirs are entitled for one share. Thus, plaintiffs as well as defendants together are entitled for 1/7th share, which has to be divided equally by metes and bounds. However, in so far as property in Sy.No.604/1A measuring 5 acres 6 guntas, which is held by the appellant herein is concerned, 1 acre 6 guntas shall be made available to the appellant herein as per his 14 preference and remaining 4 acres shall be made available for common partition by metes and bounds among other plaintiffs and defendants along with other properties held by plaintiffs and other defendants and the same has to be shared equally. 11. Since little more extent of land is given to the appellant in Sy.No.604/1A, that could be adjusted in favour of the plaintiffs and other defendants while earmarking the division by metes and bounds, by identifying the appropriate share in other properties. Accordingly, RSA No.6065/2010 is allowed and RSA No.5985/2010 is allowed in part. Sd/- JUDGE Sub/bkp
